tools.name: search_cloudflare_documentation
tools.description:
Search the Cloudflare documentation.

		This tool should be used to answer any question about Cloudflare products or features, including:
		- Workers, Pages, R2, Images, Stream, D1, Durable Objects, KV, Workflows, Hyperdrive, Queues
		- AI Search, Workers AI, Vectorize, AI Gateway, Browser Rendering
		- Zero Trust, Access, Tunnel, Gateway, Browser Isolation, WARP, DDOS, Magic Transit, Magic WAN
		- CDN, Cache, DNS, Zaraz, Argo, Rulesets, Terraform, Account and Billing

		Results are returned as semantically similar chunks to the query.

tools.name: migrate_pages_to_workers_guide
tools.description: ALWAYS read this guide before migrating Pages projects to Workers.

prompts.name: workers-prompt-full
prompts.description: Detailed prompt for generating Cloudflare Workers code (and other developer platform products) from https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/prompt.txt

results.description: Server did not advertise: name, version. Got name='unknown' version='unknown' protocol='unknown'.
results.remediation: Ensure the MCP server returns a populated 'serverInfo' object in its initialize response (name and version fields).

results.details: Error excerpt: 'MCP error -32602: Tool mcpsafe_nonexistent_tool_probe not found'

results.description: Server returns 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *' — any browser origin can connect to this MCP server.
results.details: Wildcard CORS — any browser origin can connect to this MCP server
results.remediation: Restrict the CORS origin allowlist to known, trusted domains. A wildcard allows a malicious web page to make authenticated cross-origin calls to this MCP server.

results.description: Process privilege check requires STDIO transport — skipped (transport='http').
Environment variable probe requires STDIO transport — skipped (transport='http').
Server correctly rejected all invalid tool names.
Server correctly rejected oversized tool arguments.
Server accepted a second initialize() call on an already active session. The session remained usable, but the server state may have been silently reset.
results.details:
Double initialize() succeeded.
Post-replay list_tools() succeeded — session is still functional but capabilities or internal state may have been altered.
results.remediation: The server should reject or ignore repeated initialize() calls. Accepting them enables mid-session protocol version downgrade and capability reset attacks.

results.description: Server rejected deeply nested JSON argument.
Server rejected homoglyph tool name 'seаrch_cloudflare_documentation' correctly.
Server did not negotiate a recognised MCP protocol version (version is empty or unknown).
results.remediation: Ensure the server returns a valid protocolVersion in its initialize response (e.g. '2024-11-05'). Clients rely on this to select compatible behaviour.
